commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a Mistral Monthly Plan metric option, fetches console.mistral.ai Vibe usage into an extra rate window, and changes menu-bar preference handling/localization for Mistral.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection for the PR defects: current Mistral settings/importer are admin.mistral.ai-scoped while the new fetch sends that cookie header to console.mistral.ai, and existing provider-pane coverage still expects one Mistral metric option. I did not run live Mistral probes because AGENTS.md forbids account-backed provider checks unless explicitly requested.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs maintainer security/product review and contributor real behavior proof before any automated repair or merge path.

target.title: feat(mistral): add Monthly Plan menu bar metric (subscription usage)
target.body: ## Summary Closes #1363. Adds support for Mistral's subscription (Vibe CLI / Codestral) usage in the menu bar, addressing the gap reported in the issue where only API spend was shown but not the monthly plan quota. - Adds a new **Monthly Plan** option to the Mistral "Menu bar metric" picker (alongside the renamed **Pay-as-you-go**) - Fetches `usage_percentage` from `console.mistral.ai/api-ui/trpc/billing.vibeUsage` using the same cookie already collected for the billing page - Displays the subscription usage % in the menu bar when Monthly Plan is selected (e.g. `3%`, with reset countdown) - Falls back gracefully if the vibe API is unavailable (non-fatal — existing cost display unaffected) - Exempts Mistral from the `isBalanceOnlyProvider` restriction so the new preference persists correctly ## Test plan - [ ] Enable Mistral with a Manual cookie containing `csrftoken` - [ ] Open provider settings → Mistral → Menu bar metric → select **Monthly Plan** - [ ] Verify preference sticks and menu bar shows subscription usage % with reset time - [ ] Verify **Pay-as-you-go** still shows `$X.XXXX` cost as before - [ ] Verify users without a monthly plan (vibe API fails) see no regression — app still loads cost data normally 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: lfmundim
